era: 1960-1968 original. Proto-Punk. E=8 -- maximum aggression, deliberate distortion, screaming vocals, Tacoma.

theory_nodes.concept: Proto-punk before the term existed. The Sonics in 1965: deliberate distortion, screaming vocals, confrontational energy over technical refinement. Punk's core aesthetic six to ten years before punk had a name. The genealogical evidence is the catalog: Have Love Will Travel, Psycho, The Witch, Strychnine.
theory_nodes.description: The forefathers. Playing punk in 1965. The genealogical upstream of the punk movement.

theory_nodes.concept: Three engine appearances: 221 (E=5) → 241 (E=8) → 268 (E=8, proto-punk frame). The engine's deepening engagement with The Sonics' significance. Each appearance has taken them more seriously. Correct direction of travel.
theory_nodes.description: Three appearances, three framings, one trajectory: increasing recognition of significance.

theory_nodes.concept: 'Have Love Will Travel' as cultural object: 60 years of covers, hundreds of bands. Songs that survive six decades of cover versions have achieved something real. The simplicity (riff, urgency, velocity) is the genius.
theory_nodes.description: The most-covered Sonics song as evidence of compositional durability.

cross_references.relationship: FIRST APPEARANCE at E=5. Introduction. 47-degree gap to second appearance.
SECOND APPEARANCE at E=8. Energy revised to maximum. 27-degree gap to this third appearance.
Proto-punk arc (265-268) bookends: proto-punk at E=8 at degree 265 (Capitol Hill, 1982) and proto-punk at E=8 at degree 268 (Tacoma, 1965). The arc opens and closes at maximum energy.

sps_pair.pairing_note: BIOLOGY RETURNS after 23 abiotic degrees (245-267). The Pacific Wren's 113+ note rain-day chorus pairs with The Sonics' proto-punk maximum energy. Both are maxima in their domain: the wren sings more notes than any other North American bird; The Sonics played more aggressively than any contemporary American band. Dual maxima.
